visual_features.overall_style: Early-2010s promotional site with a loud, high-contrast visual identity: neon pink and black ‘club’ aesthetic on top, combined with a light yellow-green content area. The style mixes skeuomorphic details (beveled text, drop shadows, 3D-like header) with a fixed-width, two-column layout. Overall feel is energetic but visually heavy and somewhat dated.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Primary focal point is the large black header banner with oversized artist name in a white-to-pink gradient and pink flame motifs. Secondary hierarchy level is the horizontal navigation bar directly beneath, with the active tab in a bright pink pill, drawing attention to the current section. Within the content, the left sidebar thumbnails create a strong vertical column, but the main content area on the right draws focus via a lighter background and a central title row (the video title strip). The info box on the right (with grey header ‘ESTAS VIENDO’) forms a tertiary focal area due to its bordered box and contrast vs. the yellow-green background.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is moderate but inconsistent. The header and nav are tightly packed, with minimal vertical padding. The main content area has more breathing room: generous left/right margins within the yellow-green container and moderate spacing between the tab strip, title bar, and info box. The left sidebar is visually dense with stacked thumbnails and relatively small gaps between elements, leading to a heavier feel on that side.
unique_elements: High-contrast neon pink and black hero banner with flame-like decorative shapes.
Rounded-top navigation bar with pill-shaped active tab embedded in the nav strip.
Two-tone page background separation: bold pink sidebar column vs. soft yellow-green content area.
Metadata info card with a light header band and detailed ‘video info’ micro-layout reminiscent of early video portals.
Tabbed content headings (‘Ultimos’, ‘Populares’, ‘Buscar’) styled as text-centered strips across the main content.

color_scheme._notes: Color values are approximate, inferred from the screenshot. Primary brand feel comes from neon pink (#FF007F) + black. Secondary pink (#E0006A) appears in sidebars and accents. The content background is a pale yellow-green (#F5FBB0). Neutrals include very light grey backgrounds (#F7F7F7), mid-grey borders (#DDDDDD), and grey text (#999999–#333333). Semantic colors are not explicitly present and are suggested for a modernized system.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Fixed-width, two-column layout approximating a 12-column grid where the left sidebar occupies roughly 3–4 columns (about 25–30% width) and the main content area uses the remaining 8–9 columns.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Left sidebar + main content. Header and navigation span full page width at the top, with content arranged in a two-column structure beneath.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top banner (branding) → horizontal navigation bar → main content wrapper split into left sidebar (video list) and right main panel (video player/title/info). Within the right main panel, there is a top tab strip (‘Ultimos’, ‘Populares’, ‘Buscar’), then a title/flash-player area on the left and a detailed metadata card on the right.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned grid. Logo is centered within the banner visually, but navigation links are centered horizontally across the bar. Inside the main content, titles and body text are mostly left-aligned. Tabs (‘Ultimos’, ‘Populares’, ‘Buscar’) are center-aligned within equal-width columns across the top of the main content area. Cards and boxes use consistent internal left padding and top-aligned text.
responsive_hints: Design appears fixed-width and optimized for desktop resolutions (typical of early 2010s).
No visible mobile navigation patterns (no hamburger menu or stacked nav); would likely require a separate mobile layout.
Elements such as the Flash-player placeholder and rigid sidebars suggest no intrinsic responsiveness.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom hierarchy: full-width header banner → full-width horizontal navigation bar → split content area with a tall left sidebar (stacked vertical video thumbnails and titles) and a broader right content panel. Within the right panel, content flows from a horizontal tab row at the top to a main video title and playback area (center-left) with a metadata/info card (right-aligned). The left column is effectively a vertical list of ‘video cards’, each composed of a thumbnail, bordered frame, and caption link underneath.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Header occupies roughly 15–20% of the vertical viewport, followed by a relatively slim nav bar (~5%). The remaining 75–80% is the content region: left sidebar uses about 25–30% of width, right main content about 70–75%. Inside the main panel, the upper tab strip is shallow, with most vertical real estate dedicated to the (missing) video player area and a moderately sized info card to the right. Margins between the main content’s outer edge and the browser frame are modest, creating a boxed site feel rather than fully fluid width.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate-to-high density. The left sidebar is visually dense with stacked items and minimal vertical spacing, producing a ‘list of thumbnails’ feel. The right side is less dense, with clear separation between the tab strip, title area, and info card, but still uses small type and compact line spacing, characteristic of information-dense layouts.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Visual flow starts at the bold logo in the header, then moves horizontally across the navigation bar (left-to-right). From there, the eye is pulled down to the center/right main content due to the light yellow-green panel contrasting against the darker sidebar. Within the content, the eye first hits the central video title strip, then moves right to the metadata info card (because of its clear border and header label), and finally scans down the left sidebar thumbnails. Overall pattern is a modified F-pattern: across header and nav, then down the left column, but with a strong secondary pull into the main right panel.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons are not heavily emphasized as traditional CTAs but appear as tab-like and nav-like elements. The primary navigation items are text-based links in uppercase on a dark grey bar, with the active item highlighted via a bright pink pill-shaped background (approx. 4–6px border radius) and white text. These exhibit flat design with no visible drop shadow, slight inner padding (~8–10px vertical, 12–16px horizontal). Other 'button-like' elements (e.g., small utility links) appear as plain text links with underline on hover.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards appear in two main forms: (1) Sidebar video cards with a thumbnail image framed by a white border and a dark inner outline, sitting on a light grey panel with a thin border, followed by a short red or dark link caption. Corners are square or very slightly rounded (0–2px). (2) The right-side metadata info card: a light grey/white box (#F7F7F7) with a subtle 1px border (#DDDDDD), internal padding (approx. 12–16px), and a slightly darker header strip at the top displaying status text. The layout inside is text-heavy with labels (bold or uppercase) followed by plain values, plus a small thumbnail aligned to the top-right. No heavy shadows; separation relies on borders and background contrast.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Only clear form-related element is the small ‘Buscar’ (search) tab and a text input in the info box (link field) that visually resembles an input: light background, subtle border, corner radius around 2–3px. Labels appear above inputs or inline as bold text followed by a colon. Overall, form density is compact with minimal vertical spacing between fields.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation: horizontal menu bar on a dark grey rounded-top strip that spans the content width. Each item is uppercase white text, separated by vertical ‘|’ dividers. Active item sits on a hot pink pill-shaped background with a slight border-radius and possibly a subtle gradient. The bar itself has rounded top corners and may have a faint inner shadow at the top edge, giving it a pseudo-3D look. Text size is relatively small-medium (~13–14px).

ui_patterns.visual_description: Sidebar video list is a vertical stack of media items: each list item has a rectangular thumbnail with a strong white border and darker inner frame, sitting on a bright pink sidebar background. Under each thumbnail is a short truncated title link in a contrasting color (likely red or dark pink). Spacing between list items is moderate (~12–16px) but the overall impression is dense because of the bold color and large thumbnails. Bullet points are not visible; separation is via space, differing background, and borders.

heading.visual_style: Headings appear as bold sans-serif with relatively simple shapes, likely system fonts (Arial/Helvetica). The large logo text in the header is a custom graphic using a heavy, rounded, slightly condensed display sans with bevel and gradient treatment.

body.visual_style: Body text resembles a neutral sans-serif with small size and standard stroke contrast (similar to Arial). It’s utilitarian and compact, optimized for dense information rather than editorial style.

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical internal padding for cards and panels would be 12–16px (1.5–2 × base unit). Navigation items and tabs use around 8–10px vertical and 12–16px horizontal padding.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (banner → nav → content, and between major content blocks) is approximately 16–24px. Sidebar items are separated by ~12–16px.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change background or text color on hover, with the hover state similar to the active pink pill or a lighter grey highlight.
Text links probably use underline on hover and possibly color shifts to a darker pink or blue for visited states.
Sidebar thumbnails may have a hover outline or slight border-color change to emphasize interactivity.
Tabs (‘Ultimos’, ‘Populares’, ‘Buscar’) could use a background-color change and bold text on hover/active to indicate the selected filter.

Micro-interactions are likely minimal and instantaneous (no animated transitions), but a modernized version could add 150–200ms fade or slide transitions for tab content and subtle scale/brightness changes on thumbnail hover.
